data.title: Huntington Ingalls Says New Agreements Ratified by Union Shipbuilders
data.description:
By Connor Hart

Huntington Ingalls Industries said union-represented shipbuilders ratified new collective-bargaining agreements through 2031, securing higher wages.

The ship-and-submersible builder said Thursday that all five collective bargaining units at its Ingalls Shipbuilding division ratified the agreements, which it said ensure an immediate 18% or higher base wage increase.

The increase marks the largest single wage increase in Ingalls Shipbuilding history, the company said.

Huntington Ingalls said the contracts, which provide for wage growth of 35% to 47% over the next five years, should improve recruitment and attrition, while also enabling accelerated throughput.

"The dedication and expertise of our Ingalls shipbuilders are essential to meeting the U.S. Navy's growing demand for more ships, and together we will continue advancing our mission to build the most capable ships that protect and serve our nation's warfighters," Ingalls Shipbuilding President Brian Blanchette said.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: Grenergy Wins Contracts For 760 MWh Storage Projects In UK
data.description:
Grenergy Renovables SA :

  • SECURES CAPACITY CONTRACTS FOR 760 MWH STORAGE PROJECTS IN THE UK

  • CONTRACTS TO BEGIN CONTRIBUTING TO STABILITY OF ELECTRICITY GRID FROM OCT 2029, WITH DURATION OF 15 YEARS

  • SERVICES WILL BE REMUNERATED AT A FIXED PRICE, IMPLYING FOR GRENERGY MINIMUM ECONOMIC ALLOCATION EXCEEDING 40 MILLION EUROS

  • PROJECTS EXPECT REVENUES FROM CAPACITY, ENERGY TRADING, ANCILLARY SERVICES MARKETS

data.title: Bumble's AI Matchmaker Seen Boosting Paying User Base — Market Talk
data.description: Bumble's plans for an AI matchmaker could go a long way toward convincing users to pay for the dating app, M Science analyst Chandler Willison says. "I think a ton of people would pay extra for a service where you just enter in your information and you have an agent, basically, that just finds you dates," Willison says in an interview. The tool would likely appeal most to users burnt out from swiping and fed up with judging profiles by images, he says. The feature harkens back to traditional matchmaking services, which were more popular before the rise of dating apps, he says. An AI matchmaker is "the most obvious and most useful implementation of AI that the space could work towards," he says. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

data.title: LCUT: Q4 profit surged on higher margins and cost cuts, with Dolly brand sales up 150% to $18M
data.description:
Q4 saw strong bottom line growth despite lower sales, driven by early price increases, cost reductions, and international resilience. The Dolly brand surged 150% to $18M, and SG&A fell 12%. Focus for 2026 is on top-line recovery, new products, and operational efficiency.

data.title: Bumble's Member Reset Phase Progressed Quicker than Expected — Market Talk
data.description: JP Morgan analysts say Bumble made it through the latest phase of its turnaround faster than they expected. Bumble had been working to impose more stringent requirements for users and cut down on bad actors, and with that reset in the rearview, it's now seeing metrics like registrations and active users stabilizing. The dating app is now shifting its focus to product innovation, including an AI dating assistant and profiles that de-emphasize swiping, the analysts say. "Bumble still has a long road ahead to get back to sustainable revenue growth, but we no longer think an Underweight rating is appropriate," they say, upgrading their rating on the stock to neutral. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

data.title: Benchmark Treasury yield hits five-week high as Gulf tensions escalate
data.description:
Key points:
  • Main US indexes all down ; Nasdaq off most, down >1%
  • Industrials weakest S&P 500 sector; Energy leads gainers
  • Dollar up; US crude advances >8%; bitcoin slips; gold off nearly 1%
  • US 10-Year Treasury yield rises to ~4.24%

BENCHMARK TREASURY YIELD HITS FIVE-WEEK HIGH AS GULF TENSIONS ESCALATE

U.S. Treasury yields are rising on Thursday as Iran ramps up attacks on energy and transport facilities in the Gulf, further driving oil prices up and stoking concerns about resurgent inflation that could prompt the Federal Reserve to keep interest rates higher for longer.

Meanwhile, traders are noting that after ending February in a precarious position on the charts, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield vaulted off the support line from its April 2023 low.

Indeed, since Friday, February 27, the day before the U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran, the yield has jumped more than 28 basis points in less than two weeks. On Thursday, it hit a five-week high at 4.255%, and is now about 4.24%.

With this, the yield is forging back above its 20-month moving average, which is now around 4.20%.

This can suggest the yield, within the context of its contracting multi-year range, is refocused on upside levels.

Clearing the upper weekly Ichimoku Cloud boundary around 4.255% can see the yield challenge its 4.313% January high.

The May to August 2025 highs span from 4.414% to 4.629%. The resistance line from the October 2023 high is now around 4.62% on a monthly basis.

Weakness back below 4.20% can suggest the rise is stalling. A break below 4.13% can see the yield re-threaten the support line from the April 2023 low, which is now around 3.94% on a monthly basis, as well as the March 2 low at 3.926%.

data.title: Bitcoin Slides 2% as Oil Surges 10.5% After Tanker Attacks
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) traded lower Thursday as a sudden spike in oil prices rattled risk sentiment across global markets, underscoring how geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are continuing to shape investor positioning. The largest cryptocurrency slipped as much as 2% and hovered near $70,000 early in New York after attacks on two oil tankers in Iraqi waters pushed energy markets sharply higher. Brent crude surged as much as 10.5%, briefly lifting oil back above $100 a barrel and prompting a broad risk-off move that extended into equities and digital assets.

Despite the pullback, Bitcoin has remained relatively resilient since the United States and Israel initiated a bombing campaign against Iran on Feb. 28. The token was among the first assets to fall during that weekend when traditional markets were closed before rebounding quickly. In the days that followed, Bitcoin climbed back above $73,000 as traders looked for highly liquid assets that could allow rapid repositioning during periods of heightened volatility. Rachael Lucas, an analyst at BTC Markets, noted that some profit-taking around the $70,000 level could emerge while oil volatility and geopolitical uncertainty continue to cloud the near-term macro backdrop.

Even so, some market indicators suggest the current setup could still support upside momentum if broader pressures stabilize. Andreja Cobeljic, head of derivatives trading at Amina Bank, pointed to negative funding rates on Bitcoin perpetual futures, which fell to their lowest level in nearly five weeks on Thursday. According to Cobeljic, such conditions have occurred only 10 times since 2018 and have historically preceded stronger forward returns over longer horizons. He also noted that large holders commonly known as whales have continued accumulating Bitcoin during dips into the low $60,000 range, a pattern that could potentially position the market for a relief rally even as geopolitical headlines remain unsettled.

data.title: Soybeans Climb as Cargill Halts Shipments to China — Market Talk
data.description: A temporary stoppage in Brazilian soybean shipments to China is helping to push CBOT soybean futures to their highest level since May 2024. Media reports say that agricultural trade giant Cargill has halted its purchases of Brazilian soybeans, thanks to a change in inspection rules by Brazilian officials that make scanning for pests in exports more thorough. A spokesperson for Cargill's Brazilian operations declined a request to comment further on the matter. Competition from Brazil and Argentina on the world export market have been a factor keeping a lid on CBOT soybean prices. The most-active contract rises 1.2% Thursday. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: Lilly finds impurity in compounded version of its weight-loss drug, warns of health risks
data.description:
Key points:
  • Lilly warns of impurity in compounded tirzepatide with vitamin B12, calls for recall
  • Compounders have argued products are legal and safe
  • FDA has issued warning letters to compounders for making misleading claims

By Leah Douglas

Compounded weight-loss drugs that contain vitamin B12 and the main ingredient in Eli Lilly's Zepbound could present health risks to consumers due to a previously unidentified impurity caused in the preparation, the U.S. drugmaker said in a public letter released on Thursday.

The letter, based on Lilly's testing of samples of compounded products, is the company's latest move against drug compounders it says are marketing illegal copies of Zepbound and its diabetes drug Mounjaro. Both medicines have the same active ingredient, tirzepatide.

Lilly has sued compounders, wellness centers and other companies for selling products claiming to contain tirzepatide.

Compounders have argued their products are legal under a narrow provision of federal law that allows compounding when patients require personalization due to medical concerns, like the addition of vitamins or doses not available in the branded versions.

Lilly's testing of products obtained from compounding pharmacies, medspas and telehealth networks found "significant levels of an impurity that results from a chemical reaction between tirzepatide and B12," according to the letter and a scientific manuscript on the testing process shared with Reuters.

The impurity was identified in all 10 samples it tested, the company said.

"FDA warns that compounded products can be risky for patients because they are not reviewed for safety, effectiveness, or quality," Eli Lilly Chief Medical Officer David Hyman said in a statement. "Adding a reactive substance like vitamin B12 without clinical testing or FDA review introduces additional unknown risks."

Lilly said it notified the U.S. Food and Drug Administration of its findings and called for a nationwide recall of products containing both ingredients.

Scott Brunner, CEO of the lobby group Alliance for Pharmacy Compounding, said Lilly's findings were concerning but that the company would need to provide details of its sampling process, how the tested samples were obtained and other specifics to fully assess any health risks to consumers.

"Based on what I’ve read so far, Lilly isn't telling us enough to make that determination," Brunner said.

The FDA in September issued warning letters to 30 telehealth companies for making false or misleading claims about compounded versions of weight-loss drugs.

The agency also threatened action against "illegal copycat drugs" after telehealth company Hims & Hers Health said in February it would begin selling its own version of Novo Nordisk's Wegovy pill.

Hims quickly backed away from its compounded pill and announced plans this week to sell Novo's Wegovy and Ozempic on its platform.

data.title: Adobe Is Latest Software Company Under Scrutiny Amid AI Debate — Earnings Preview
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Adobe is scheduled to report its first-quarter financial results after the market closes on Thursday. Here is what to know.

REVENUE: The software company is expected to report $6.28 billion in sales, compared with $5.71 billion in the previous first quarter, according to FactSet.

NET PROFIT: The company behind Photoshop and Acrobat is expected to post a profit of $1.89 billion, compared with a profit of $1.81 billion the year before, according to FactSet.

ADJUSTED EARNINGS PER SHARE: Adjusted earnings are expected to come in at $5.87 a share, compared with $5.08 a share the prior year.

The stock has fallen 23% over the past three months, and recently traded at $270.32.

WHAT TO WATCH:

  • The key question in investors minds continues to be AI monetization. Wall Street will be watching Adobe's report to see if its AI product Firefly and other AI capabilities drive incremental revenue through pricing, Third Bridge analyst Dyland Koehler said.
  • Investors have already been tough on Adobe in recent quarters, but Wall Street has even more doubts about the software sector heading into the company's first-quarter report. Concerns that AI products will replace software tools have weighed on smaller companies like UiPath this week, while bigger enterprises including Oracle have managed to dispel worries.
  • Adobe's response to the AI disruption has been to integrate third-party models directly into its products like Photoshop and Premiere Pro. Koehler said the debate is shifting from who has the best AI model to who owns the professional workflow where those models are deployed at scale. "Our experts say Adobe's advantage lies less in raw model performance and more in enterprise trust and workflow integration," Koehler said.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: First US Bancshares 2025: $59.4M Interest Income, $1.00 Diluted EPS
data.description:
First US Bancshares reported total interest income of $59.415 million and net income of $5.992 million for 2025, with diluted earnings per share of $1.00, reflecting higher provisions and operating expenses that weighed on profitability. Net interest income was $37.458 million, supported by loan and investment growth, while non-interest income contributed $3.579 million. The results include a materially higher provision for credit losses that drove year-over-year net income decline versus 2024.

Financial Highlights

  • Total interest income: $59.415 million (2025)
  • Net interest income: $37.458 million (2025)
  • Non-interest income: $3.579 million (2025)
  • Income before income taxes: $7.936 million (2025)
  • Net income: $5.992 million (2025) — declined from $8.170 million in 2024
  • Basic net income per share: $1.03 (2025)
  • Diluted net income per share: $1.00 (2025)
  • Non-interest expense (operating expense): $29.070 million (2025), up $0.714 million (2.5%) vs. 2024
  • Provision for credit losses: $4.031 million (2025), increased materially from $0.622 million in 2024

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and drivers: Net interest income rose 3.6% year-over-year driven by growth in loans and investment assets despite margin compression.
  • Loan channel shift: Management expanded indirect consumer lending, which materially increased that portfolio and was associated with higher charge-offs.
  • Deposit mix: Customer behavior shifted toward money market and savings accounts and brokered deposits, supporting liquidity and funding stability.
  • Asset quality and provisioning: Nonperforming assets declined materially, but higher provisions reflected specific commercial charge-offs and losses in the indirect loan portfolio.
  • Operational resilience: The company maintained strong core deposit funding, expanded its share repurchase authorization, and employed active interest rate hedging to manage rate risk.

Original SEC Filing: FIRST US BANCSHARES, INC. [ FUSB ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: CF Bankshares 10-K: $120.0M Interest Income, $2.69 Diluted EPS
data.description:
CF Bankshares reported solid full-year results with total interest income of $120.0 million and diluted earnings per common share of $2.69, up from $2.06 a year ago. Net income rose to $17.541 million, a 31% increase, supported by an 18% gain in net interest income to $55.0 million driven by higher loan yields and asset growth. Operating income before taxes improved to $21.527 million as the company benefited from lower interest expense and disciplined credit provisioning.

Financial Highlights

  • Total interest income: $120.0 million (interest-driven revenue up $1.6M, 1.4% vs prior year)
  • Net interest income: $55.0 million (increased $8.4M, 18.0% due to lower interest expense and asset growth)
  • Operating income (income before income taxes): $21.527 million (up $5.383M vs prior year)
  • Net income: $17.541 million (up $4.154M, 31.0% vs prior year)
  • Diluted earnings per common share: $2.69 (increased from $2.06 last year)

Business Highlights

  • Revenue momentum: Net interest income rose 18% year-over-year, driven by higher loan yields and growth in interest-earning assets.
  • Loan portfolio shift: Growth in commercial real estate and multi-family loan balances offset declines in commercial & industrial and single-family mortgage balances.
  • Deposit and funding trends: Core deposits grew modestly with increases in interest-bearing accounts and steady retail digital engagement via online and mobile channels.
  • Credit and risk management: Allowance for credit losses maintained around 1.01% under targeted CECL modeling; elevated commercial charge-offs influenced provisioning decisions.
  • Operational investments: Increased staffing and higher professional fees to strengthen underwriting, credit oversight, and digital and treasury services capabilities.

Original SEC Filing: CF BANKSHARES INC. [ CFBK ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Adobe sued for trademark infringement over 'Foundry' AI tool
data.description:
By Blake Brittain

British visual-effects software company The Foundry Visionmongers has sued Adobe in California federal court, arguing Adobe's Firefly Foundry suite of generative artificial intelligence tools violates its trademark rights in the "Foundry" name.

Foundry, owned by tech company Roper Technologies , said in the complaint filed Wednesday that Adobe's AI-based content creation software will create consumer confusion with its similar products.

Spokespeople for Adobe did not immediately respond to a request for comment on the lawsuit on Thursday. Foundry declined to comment.

The case is the latest in a raft of trademark lawsuits tied to artificial intelligence company names or products amid the current AI industry boom.

Foundry, founded in 1996, said that its software has been used in the making of every film that has won an Academy Award for visual effects since 2007. Its lawsuit said that its content-creation software, like Adobe's, has AI capabilities.

Adobe announced its Firefly Foundry software last year, which uses AI to generate text, video, images and other content. The lawsuit said Adobe was likely to cause confusion by using the "Foundry" name to sell similar software to similar markets.

Foundry requested a court order blocking Adobe from using the "Foundry" name and an unspecified amount of monetary damages.

The case is The Foundry Visionmongers Ltd v. Adobe Inc, U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California, No. 5:26-cv-02121.

For Foundry: Mark Comtois, Nicole McLaughlin and Jennifer Lantz of Duane Morris

For Adobe: attorney information not yet available

data.title: Norwegian Air loses tax case, will appeal to supreme court
data.description:
Norwegian Air Shuttle ASA :

  • WILL APPEAL EU ETS RULING TO SUPREME COURT

  • COURT OF APPEAL HAS TODAY RENDERED ITS DECISION IN PROCEEDINGS. COURT UPHELD MINISTRY'S APPEAL, FINDING THAT NORWEGIAN WAS OBLIGED TO FULFIL ITS EU ETS OBLIGATIONS FOR 2020 IN FULL, IRRESPECTIVE OF RECONSTRUCTION

  • COURT ALSO RULED THAT PENALTY IMPOSED FOR FAILURE TO MEET EU ETS OBLIGATIONS FOR 2020 WAS VALID

  • JUDGEMENT FROM COURT OF APPEAL DOES NOT ALIGN WITH JUDGEMENT OF OSLO DISTRICT COURT, NOR WITH VIEWS OF OTHER STAKEHOLDERS AND EXPERTS

  • WE ARE DISAPPOINTED AND SURPRISED

  • DOES NOT EXPECT TO MAKE ANY ADDITIONAL PROVISIONS FOLLOWING VERDICT FROM COURT OF APPEAL

data.title: Stellantis exploring deals for Chinese rivals to invest in its business, Bloomberg reports
data.description:
Stellantis is exploring deals with Chinese carmakers whereby they would invest in the Fiat owner's struggling European operations, Bloomberg News reported on Thursday.

The carmaker's executives have met with China's Xiaomi and Xpeng Inc to discuss options for an overhaul of Stellantis in Europe, including for the Chinese companies to acquire stakes in Maserati or other brands, the report said, citing people familiar with the matter.

"As part of its normal course of business, Stellantis holds discussions with a range of industry players around the world on various topics, always with the ultimate aim of providing customers with the best mobility choices," a Stellantis spokesperson told Reuters.

The proposed overhaul might eventually lead to a further separation between Stellantis' U.S. and European arms, the report said, adding that a full breakup is not the focus of current discussions.

"Stellantis states in the most categoric terms that there is no truth in the suggestion that it is considering a plan to split the Company. Any assertion to the contrary is pure invention," the spokesperson said.

Xpeng and Xiaomi did not immediately respond to requests for comment. Reuters could not verify the report.

Western automakers have been juggling investment between electric vehicles and petrol models while contending with fast-rising Chinese rivals and higher trade barriers.

Stellantis, created through the merger of Fiat Chrysler and Peugeot maker PSA, last month announced 22.2 billion euros ($26.4 billion) in charges, scaling back its EV ambitions as Europe waters down emissions targets in a move that would allow combustion engines to stay around for longer.

Several Chinese carmakers have made moves to set up production in Europe. Chinese automaker Leapmotor will build vehicles at a Stellantis plant in Spain as part of a joint venture.
